title: FTX law firm Fenwick & West to pay $54M to victims in settlement
summary: The law firm agreed to a settlement in February 2026 and is facing a separate $525 million lawsuit over its role in the collapse of the FTX crypto exchange.

title: Tom Lee’s Ethereum portfolio down $7.35B as ETH price outlook worsens
summary: ETH’s bearish chart setup points to a 25% drop toward $1,600, risking over $10 billion in paper losses for BitMine in the coming weeks.

title: Paz Says Protests Test Democracy Ahead of Talks
summary: Bolivia President Rodrigo Paz tells Wall Street Week why pragmatism — not ideology — will define his government, and why closing the chapter on 20 years of one-party rule won’t be easy. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Supertanker With Iraq Crude Exits Persian Gulf as Talks Continue
summary: A supertanker hauling Iraqi crude to China has left the Persian Gulf and crossed the US blockade line into the Arabian Sea, as talks continue to end the war between the US and Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Strategy Now Holds $65 Billion in Bitcoin—These Are Its Biggest BTC Buys
summary: How did Michael Saylor's firm amass a record stash of Bitcoin? Here's a look back at how Strategy made such massive gains.

title: AI Startup Says It Will Pay People $2,000 a Month to Masturbate—Yes, Really
summary: Joi AI is hiring 10 “masturbation consultants” to test its AI-guided masturbation feature and report how it affects stress, sleep, mood, and confidence.

title: Vitalik Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will be a ‘smaller ship,’ sell less ETH amid researcher exodus
summary: Buterin said the EF is choosing "longevity over breadth," selling less ETH and focusing exclusively on Ethereum's "CROPS" properties.

title: StablR’s EURR and USDR depeg after attacker mints $13.5 million in unbacked tokens through multisig exploit
summary: EURR fell to $0.85 and USDR fell as low as $0.40 after an attacker dumped roughly $10.4 million in face value on decentralized exchanges.

title: Trump says US will not ‘rush into a deal’ with Iran as talks continue
summary: Comments downplay hopes of an imminent reopening of the Strait of Hormuz

title: Egypt deploys jets to UAE as Iran war strains Arab alliances
summary: Abu Dhabi had hit out at countries in region for not doing more to help defend against Iranian attacks
